metadata.title:
PETITIONS LEFT


WITH UNIONS

MAN SELECTED TO CIRCULATE
REFERENDUM REQUEST HAS
CITY JOB, AND MAlONEY IS
TOO BUSY.

pages.raw_text:
1t is up to each labor union in
the city to get its members to sign
the petition for the initiative and
referendum charter amendment In
order to secure the endorsement
of sufficient number of the voters
before the next city election. This
is the announcement made today
by Thomas Maloney, chairman of
the legislative committee of the
Spokane Central Labor union.


Mr.. Feeney, elected at the last
meeting of the Central Labor cOun-
Gil to take full charge of the circu-
lation of the petitions, has been
given job with the city as sewer
inspector and has not sufficient
time to take up the matters en-
trusted to him. Mr.. Maloney is
buried in other work, and states he
has come to the conclusion that
the best way to overcome the diff
Guilty is to have a petition circu-
lated among the members of each
union in the city and to pick up
all the outside names possible.


About 1900 voters must have
signed this petition before the
registration books are closed, else
the amendment to the city charter
will be lost. Mr.. Maloney stated
today that he believed that if each
union would take hold of the cir.
culation of the petitions in earnest
their object can easily be realized.
